passages.passage_text: Directions: In each question below is given a statement followed by two assumptions numbered I and II. An assumption is something supposed or taken for granted. You have to consider the statement and the following assumptions and decide which of the assumptions is implicit in the statement. Give answer (a) if only assumption I is implicit; (b) if only assumption II is implicit; (c) if either I or II is implicit; (d) if neither I nor II is implicit; and (e) if both I and II are implicit.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: The boy is too honest not to speak the truth. Assumptions: I. Very honest boys also tell lies. II. Dishonest boys also speak the truth.

choices.a: if only assumption I is implicit
choices.b: if only assumption II is implicit
choices.c: if either I or II is implicit
choices.d: if neither I nor II is implicit
choices.e: if both I and II are implicit

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: "X air-conditioner - the largest selling range" - an advertisement. Assumptions: I. X air-conditioner is the only one with wide variations. II. There is demand for air-conditioners in the market.

choices.a: if only assumption I is implicit
choices.b: if only assumption II is implicit
choices.c: if either I or II is implicit
choices.d: if neither I nor II is implicit
choices.e: if both I and II are implicit

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: "To buy a TV X, contact Y, the sole agent of X TV." - an advertisement. Assumptions: I. People generally prefer to buy TV through sole agent. II. The TV companies do not sell their products directly.

choices.a: if only assumption I is implicit
choices.b: if only assumption II is implicit
choices.c: if either I or II is implicit
choices.d: if neither I nor II is implicit
choices.e: if both I and II are implicit

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: Opening a school in this area will be a wastage. Assumptions: I. Inhabitants of Ranchi are illiterate. II. Inhabitants of Ranchi are not interested in reading.

choices.a: if only assumption I is implicit
choices.b: if only assumption II is implicit
choices.c: if either I or II is implicit
choices.d: if neither I nor II is implicit
choices.e: if both I and II are implicit

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: It is through participatory management policy alone that industrial peace can be contained and a quality of life ensured to the worker. Assumptions: I. Quality of life in our industry is better. II. Industrial peace results in a poor quality of life.

choices.a: if only assumption I is implicit
choices.b: if only assumption II is implicit
choices.c: if either I or II is implicit
choices.d: if neither I nor II is implicit
choices.e: if both I and II are implicit

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: Please consult me before making any decision on exports from the company. Assumptions: I. You may take a wrong decision if you don't consult me. II. It is important to take a right decision.

choices.a: if only assumption I is implicit
choices.b: if only assumption II is implicit
choices.c: if either I or II is implicit
choices.d: if neither I nor II is implicit
choices.e: if both I and II are implicit

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: Warning: Cigarette smoking is injurious to health. Assumptions: I. Non-smoking promotes health. II. Really, this warning is not necessary.

choices.b: if only assumption II is implicit
choices.c: if either I or II is implicit
choices.d: if neither I nor II is implicit
choices.e: if both I and II are implicit

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: Children are influenced more by their teachers nowadays. Assumptions: I. Children consider teachers as their models. II. A large amount of children's time is spent at school.

choices.b: if only assumption II is implicit
choices.c: if either I or II is implicit
choices.d: if neither I nor II is implicit

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: For the entertainment value of television, its educational value cannot be ignored. Assumptions: I. People see television as a means of entertainment only. II. The educational value of television is not realised properly.

choices.b: if only assumption II is implicit
choices.c: if either I or II is implicit

choices.e: if both I and II are implicit

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: "If you trouble me, I will slap you." - A mother warns her child. Assumptions: I. With the warning, the child may stop troubling her. II. All children are basically naughty.

choices.b: if only assumption II is implicit
choices.c: if either I or II is implicit
choices.d: if neither I nor II is implicit
choices.e: if both I and II are implicit

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: "X air is pollution free." - an advertisement. Assumptions: I. All types of vehicles are available on rent. II. People will respond to the advertisement.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: Of all the radio sets manufactured in India, 'X' brand has the largest sale. Assumptions: I. The sale of all the radio sets manufactured in India is known. II. The manufacturing of no other radio set in India is as large as 'X' brand radio.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: As advised B to C, "Go to Jammu via Amritsar - the shortest route." Assumptions: I. B wishes to go to Jammu. II. A gives advice to everybody.

choices.c: if either I or II is implicit

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: "Do not lean out of the door of the bus." - a warning in a school bus. Assumptions: I. Leaning out of a running bus is dangerous. II. Children do not pay any heed to such warnings.
